description:
'''Grungegaze''' (also known as '''dreamo''') is a [[subgenre]] of [[rock music]] that grew out of [[soft grunge (music)|soft grunge]]. It is characterized by the use of [[shoegaze]] use of [[effects units]], [[emo]]-inspired melodies and [[grunge]]'s [[dynamic (music)|dynamic]] songwriting, using both soft reverb-heavy guitars during verses and crescendos of [[List of distortion pedals#fuzz pedals|fuzz]] during choruses.

During the 2010s Soft grunge has been closely related to the [[Shoegaze#Developments in the hardcore scene|hardcore scene's 2010s shoegaze revival]], with both styles sharing musical characteristics and sometimes being conflated by fans and critics. Grungegaze originated in the mid-2010s, when soft grunge bands embraced the influence of shoegaze, with the debut releases by [[Cloakroom (band)|Cloakroom]], [[Narrow Head]] and Nevermind Me. The genre was further popularized during the 2020s by American bands Narrow Head, [[Fleshwater]] and [[Glare (band)|Glare]], and British bands [[Oversize (band)|Oversize]] and [[Split Chain]]. By 2024, it was one of the most dominant sounds in shoegaze. It was widely influential upon shoegaze in the 2020s, helping to develop the genres [[nu-gaze (2020s)|nu-gaze]] and [[zoomergaze]].
